title: Paspalum humifusum
intro: Paspalum humifusum, also known as P. humifusum, is a perennial grass species belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as digestive issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of P. humifusum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, P. humifusum is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and it is also used to make tea and infusions, which are commonly used to treat various health concerns.
taxonomy: Paspalum humifusum is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Paspalum. This species is native to South America and is often found growing in wetlands and grasslands. Paspalum humifusum is a type of perennial grass that is known for its ability to thrive in a variety of environments. Within the Poaceae family, it is placed in the subfamily Panicoideae.
